engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Logan Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Logan in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Logan K9K engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector wear - high-pressure injectors degrade from 80k miles, causing rough idle and misfires.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow, triggering limp mode and power loss.
Turbo actuator failure - variable geometry vanes stick, causing boost issues and smoke.

cards.altText: Renault Logan K7M engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - chain tensioner wear causes rattling noise from 90k+ miles.
Oil consumption increase - valve stem seals harden, leading to blue smoke on high-mileage units.

cards.altText: Renault Logan K4M engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure - belt slip on high-mileage units if service intervals are missed.
Coil pack failure - misfires and rough running common after 70k miles on city-driven examples.

commonProblems.h2: Renault Logan Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Logan's K7M petrol and K9K diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries. While generally robust, high-mileage units can suffer timing chain wear, injector failure, or EGR issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: K9K Diesel Injector Failure - Why Logan 1.5 dCi Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: High-pressure piezo injectors degrade internally, causing poor atomisation, rough idle, and excessive fuel consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector with reconditioned unit, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; other injectors likely to follow within 12 months

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors with reconditioned units, full coding and calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance; viable if engine otherwise healthy under £1,500 car value

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Logans; avoids cascading component failures

problems.recommendation: On a Logan valued under £1,500, injector repair costs often exceed the car's worth. A rebuilt K9K engine from £495 (supply only) typically offers better long-term value and reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K9K engine prices for your Renault Logan

problems.h4: K7M Timing Chain Rattle - Why Logan 1.6 Petrol Engines Develop Noise
problems.rootCause: Chain tensioner hydraulic pressure drops with age, allowing chain stretch and guide wear, causing metallic rattling on cold start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner and inspect guides; chain not replaced
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix; stretched chain may still cause issues within 6-12 months

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, and sprockets; full timing reset
repairOptions.longevity: Restores reliability; viable if cylinder head and bottom end are sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective for Logans over 100k miles; eliminates all internal wear concerns

problems.recommendation: If your Logan has over 100k miles and shows chain noise, a rebuilt K7M engine from £425 (supply only) often costs less than a full timing kit repair and delivers like-new reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K7M timing chain replacement or engine swap

problems.h4: EGR Valve Clogging - Why Logan Diesel Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Renault Logan 1.5 dCi (2004-2012, all K9K codes)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve accumulates carbon deposits, restricting flow and triggering fault codes, reduced power, and black smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean EGR valve and passages, reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary; carbon will re-accumulate if driving pattern unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned or new EGR valve, full coding and adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable fix; recommended if valve is physically damaged or seized

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated EGR components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage Logans with multiple diesel system faults

problems.recommendation: For Logans under 90k miles, EGR cleaning or replacement is cost-effective. For higher mileage units with additional issues (injectors, turbo), a rebuilt engine from £495 offers better overall value.
problems.cta: Compare K9K engine prices including EGR-ready rebuilt units

problems.h4: Turbo Actuator Failure - Why Logan 1.5 dCi Loses Boost
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo actuator arm seizes due to carbon buildup, preventing vane movement and causing under/over-boost faults.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace actuator arm, recalibrate turbo vanes
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo internals are healthy; temporary if vanes are carboned

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned turbocharger with new actuator, full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution; recommended if turbo shows shaft play or oil leaks

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most economical for Logans with turbo failure plus other diesel system issues

problems.recommendation: If your Logan has turbo failure plus injector or EGR issues, a rebuilt K9K engine from £495 (supply only) typically costs less than repairing multiple components separately.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K turbo-ready rebuilt engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Logan
finalCta.paragraph: The problems above show why many Logan owners face repair bills exceeding their car's value. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Logan specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned, or used engines. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide delivery and supply & fit options, you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Logan Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Logan and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Renault Logan 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L dCi diesel was the backbone of the Logan diesel range from 2004-2012, offered in multiple power outputs across Authentique to Privilege trims and the MCV estate. Its common-rail injection delivered strong economy but required careful maintenance to avoid injector and EGR issues.
commonFailurePoints: turbo actuator sticking at higher mileage

items.description: The entry-level 1.4L petrol powered early Authentique and Expression Logans from launch, offering simple, affordable motoring with chain-driven reliability but prone to oil consumption on high-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Valve stem seal wear causing blue smoke
ignition coil failure leading to misfires

engineYears.h2: Renault Logan Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Renault Logan's UK production from 2004-2012, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Logan launched in the UK with simple, affordable petrol and diesel engines on the new B0 platform shared with Dacia.
keyChanges: UK market launch with Renault and Dacia badging
Initial engines: 1.4L K7J petrol, 1.6L K7M petrol, 1.5L K9K diesel
Authentique and Expression trim levels introduced
knownFor: Simple, robust engineering focused on low-cost ownership
Early K9K injectors sensitive to fuel quality and maintenance
Strong parts commonality with Clio III and Kangoo
High mileage examples now dominate replacement enquiries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2006 Renault Logan replacement engines
years.ticker: Logan 1.4 engine replacement • K7M timing chain noise • K9K injector failure cost

years.description: The 2007 update added the 1.6L 16V petrol and higher-output dCi variants, expanding the Logan's appeal to fleet and MCV estate buyers.
keyChanges: Introduction of 1.6L 16V K4M petrol engine for Privilege trim
New 86hp K9K 796 diesel variant for improved performance
MCV estate variant launched with extended load space
Dynamique trim added mid-range equipment level
knownFor: K4M 16V offers stronger performance but requires belt maintenance
K9K 796 became the most searched diesel replacement code
MCV variants popular with tradesmen; higher mileage accumulation
EGR and turbo issues more common on post-2007 dCi units
years.cta: Compare prices for 2007-2009 Renault Logan engine replacement
years.ticker: Logan MCV engine swap • K4M timing belt replacement • K9K 796 turbo failure

years.description: The Logan's final UK years saw refined engine mapping and the introduction of the 90hp dCi, but sales volumes declined as focus shifted to the Duster.
keyChanges: Introduction of 90hp K9K 612 diesel for late Privilege/MCV models
Detuned 84hp K7M 800 petrol variant for cost-conscious buyers
Gradual phase-out of Renault-badged Logans in favour of Dacia
Final UK registrations in 2012 before model discontinuation
knownFor: Late-model Logans often lower mileage but still value-sensitive
K9K 612 shares failure patterns with earlier K9K variants
Pickup variants popular with trades; higher wear on diesel units
Replacement demand now driven by high-mileage commercial use
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Renault Logan replacement engines
years.ticker: Logan 90hp dCi replacement • K7M 800 engine code • Logan Pickup diesel engine

faq.h2: Renault Logan Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Renault Logan engine?
items.answer: Yes, if you find a used or rebuilt engine under £500-£800. The Logan's low residual value means expensive repairs rarely make sense, but a budget replacement can extend life cost-effectively.
keyPoints: Used K7M/K9K engines available from £295-£795 supply only
Rebuilt units from £425 offer upgraded reliability with warranty
Fitting costs £350-£650; compare total cost vs car value
Ideal for Logans in good body/chassis condition under £1,500 value
items.cta: Compare total replacement costs for your Renault Logan

items.question: What is the most reliable Renault Logan engine?
items.answer: The 1.6L 8V petrol K7M 710 is generally the most trouble-free, with simple chain-driven design and strong parts availability across the B0 platform.
keyPoints: K7M timing chain is robust but tensioner can wear after 90k miles
Avoid early K9K diesel units if you do mostly short journeys
1.6L 16V K4M offers more power but needs belt maintenance
Always verify service history regardless of engine choice
items.cta: Find reliable rebuilt K7M engines for your Logan

items.question: How much does a Renault Logan engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt engine prices range from £395 for a 1.4L petrol K7J to £1,450 for a late 1.5L dCi K9K 612, supply only; fitting adds £350-£650.
keyPoints: Used engines start around £295 but carry shorter warranties
Reconditioned units balance cost and reliability at £425-£1,250
Supply & fit packages available from £795 total for budget options
Always get multiple quotes; prices vary by engine code and location
items.cta: Get instant quotes for your exact Logan engine code

items.question: Will a Renault Clio engine fit my Logan?
items.answer: Yes, many B0 platform engines are interchangeable; Clio III, Sandero, Modus and Kangoo units with matching codes (K7M, K9K, K4M) will fit your Logan with minor adaptations.
keyPoints: Verify engine code match first (e.g., K7M 710 to K7M 710)
ECU coding may be required for post-2007 models with immobiliser
Mounting points and ancillaries are largely shared across B0 platform
Using a more common Clio engine can improve availability and reduce cost
items.cta: Check B0 platform compatibility for your Logan engine

items.question: What causes loss of power in a Renault Logan 1.5 dCi?
items.answer: Most commonly EGR valve clogging, turbo actuator sticking, or injector wear on the K9K engine; diagnosis is essential before committing to expensive repairs.
keyPoints: EGR cleaning or replacement costs £125-£545 specialist pricing
Turbo actuator repair £195-£375; full turbo £495-£995
Four new injectors can cost £800-£1,200 vs rebuilt engine from £495
Get a proper diagnostic scan before deciding on repair vs replacement
items.cta: Compare K9K engine prices after diagnosing power loss

items.question: Does the Renault Logan 1.6 petrol have a timing belt or chain?
items.answer: The 1.6L 8V K7M uses a timing chain (generally maintenance-free but tensioner can wear); the 1.6L 16V K4M uses a timing belt requiring replacement every 60k miles or 5 years.
keyPoints: K7M chain rattle on cold start indicates tensioner wear from 90k miles
K4M belt failure can cause catastrophic engine damage if neglected
Belt replacement costs £245-£495; chain tensioner £245-£425
Always confirm your engine code before ordering replacement parts
items.cta: Get quotes for K7M or K4M timing component replacement

items.question: How long should a Renault Logan engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, K7M petrol engines can reach 150k+ miles; K9K diesel units often achieve 120k-180k miles but are sensitive to injector and EGR maintenance.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes critical for K9K injector longevity
Timing component checks recommended at 90k miles for peace of mind
Short-journey driving accelerates DPF/EGR issues on diesel models
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can match or exceed original lifespan
items.cta: Find long-life rebuilt engines for your Renault Logan

items.question: Can I upgrade my Renault Logan engine?
items.answer: Physical swaps are possible within the B0 platform (e.g., K7M to K4M) but require ECU coding, wiring adaptations, and may affect insurance; often not cost-effective for low-value Logans.
keyPoints: Stick to same-fuel, same-code replacements for simplest fitment
Performance upgrades rarely justify cost on budget Logan models
Ensure any swap complies with MOT emissions and noise regulations
Consult a specialist before attempting non-standard engine installations
items.cta: Enquire about compatible Logan engine upgrade options

items.question: What is the best used engine to buy for a Renault Logan?
items.answer: A low-mileage K7M 710 or K9K 796 from a breaker with compression test results and at least a 3-month warranty offers the best balance of cost and reliability.
keyPoints: Verify engine code matches your original unit exactly
Request photos of the running engine or compression test report
Confirm warranty terms and return policy before payment
Factor in delivery costs; local breakers may offer better total value
items.cta: Compare used Logan engine availability and warranty terms

items.question: Do I need to replace the ECU when fitting a replacement Logan engine?
items.answer: Usually not; most Logan ECUs are not engine-locked, but post-2007 models with immobiliser may require coding to recognise the replacement engine's sensors.
keyPoints: Pre-2007 Logans typically plug-and-play for same-code engines
Post-2007 may need diagnostic tool to reset immobiliser adaptation
Specialist fitters can handle coding as part of supply & fit service
Always retain your original ECU and keys during engine replacement
items.cta: Find Logan engine specialists who handle ECU coding
